notes: Master-Reorg Ship B3 (router gating, part 3 of 4) — gate showOpsTab's push off the legacy p:'sop'. Redesign-only, legacy byte-identical. showOpsTab is the ops-tab engine; its single nav_push (the choke point for all direct callers — the Rack Map button, deleteSOP's return, burndown job complete/archive, audit actions) planted {p:'sop', o:tab} with NO d: key, so on back-nav the nav_restore top-guard (which only redirects the bare triage-root) fell through to showPage('sop', tnEl, o) — and showPage fires showOpsTab ONLY for id==='sop' (line 16736), painting the legacy #ops-tab-strip = the leak. FIX: the push is now p:(redesign_isOn() ? 'work' : 'sop'). Under redesign a real showOpsTab action pushes {p:'work', o:tab}; on restore showPage('work', null, o) just activates #pg-work (verified: showPage only calls showOpsTab for id==='sop', so no strip is painted and the o arg is inert for 'work') — no leak. The push line always calls nav_push, which self-suppresses during showPage/popstate restore (via _navInternalCall/_navSuppressPush), so this change only affects the direct-action push. Under ?legacy=1 redesign_isOn() is false → still pushes p:'sop' and drives the #ops-tab-strip exactly as today → byte-identical. KNOWN restore nuance (acceptable, not a regression vs the leak it replaces): backing into a completed ops-action state now lands on the redesign Work page rather than re-rendering that specific ops tab (there is no d: to re-dispatch the tool) — no legacy strip, no crash; these are rare edge-case back-navs into transient action states. This closes the showOpsTab direct-push leak class; the LAST piece (B4 — the central showPage()-level guard for stale history + the live Ref-search-bar direct showPage(legacy) leak + host-hardening) is HELD for John's device-verify (highest blast radius). No new assets; CSS unchanged (braces 3560); 3-stamp lockstep; gates green (4 blocks compile, CRLF preserved). DEVICE-VERIFY (HARD-STOP, clear SW cache first): under the DEFAULT (redesign) URL — complete/archive a burndown job, delete a SOP, open Rack Map from a Deploy button, finish an audit; press Back after each and confirm NO legacy #ops-tab-strip pill row flashes (you land in the redesign frame). Then ?legacy=1: exercise the same actions and confirm the #ops-tab-strip pills behave identically. ROLLBACK: revert the commit.
